title: Housekeeping Assistance * Older Adults - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Housekeeping Assistance * Older Adults 24 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Older Adults Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness AGE+ CIRCLES OF CARE THE DALLES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Matches volunteers with older adults to provide assistance such as friendly visits or phone calls, transportation to medical appointments, grocery shopping, light housework, and yard work. Volunteer opportunities are also available. Eligibility: Supportive services: Age 55 and older Volunteers: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (503) 408-0724 Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON HERMISTON OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care to older adults living in their homes. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, and meal preparation. Also offers case management to help older adults make cost-effective and appropriate decisions regarding their care needs. Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and local funding amounts. Hours may range from 5-20 hours per week. Some examples of when Oregon Project Independence may be appropriate: -When an individual requires personal and home care assistance due to injury or illness -When an individual has been hospitalized and needs help while recovering in their home 1565 N 1st Street Suite 1 Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: -Age 60 and older, and individuals of any age diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease or a related disorder -Ages 19-59 individuals who have physical disabilities Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care to older adults living in their homes. The program is volunteer-driven and professionally supported. Volunteer opportunities are available. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Companion Care: For individuals aged 65 and older who live independently in their homes and are socially isolated, with no significant cognitive impairment. Services include Oregon Project Independence Services (OPI) and community resources. Additionally offers short-term case management (Options Counseling) and chronic disease management. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 60 and older with a focus on the African-American community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY DISABILITY, AGING AND VETERAN SERVICES OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care to qualifying older adults living in their homes. Services can include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, and meal preparation. Also offers case management to help older adults make cost-effective and appropriate decisions regarding their care needs. Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and local funding amounts. Hours generally range from 15-20 hours a month. Some examples of when Oregon Project Independence may be appropriate: -When an individual requires personal and home care assistance due to physical disability or cognitive impairment -As a respite
